stories.title: CPSC Recalls BBRKIN Biometric Gun Safes — Faulty Lock Can Be Opened by Unauthorized Users

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
RECALL (July 9): BBRKIN biometric gun safes. Lock can be opened by unauthorized users — risk of death. 9,100 units on Amazon.

Stop biometric use. Remove batteries. Use key only. Free repair kit available. 🚨 #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
If you have a BBRKIN gun safe (model QHXP029B):

→ Stop the biometric lock
→ Remove batteries
→ Use key only
→ Get a free repair kit from BBRKIN

No injuries reported — but faulty biometrics + firearms = real risk. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If your household has a gun safe with a biometric fingerprint lock from BBRKIN (or the brand MouTec), there's a safety action you need to take today.

The CPSC recalled 9,100 of these safes on July 9 — model number QHXP029B — after finding that the biometric lock can be opened by unauthorized users. The hazard the agency identifies is serious: if the lock can't reliably secure the safe against unauthorized access, the firearms stored inside are potentially accessible to anyone in the home, including children. The CPSC classifies this as a serious injury hazard and risk of death.

The good news is you don't have to stop using the safe entirely. The CPSC's remedy is clear and specific: stop relying on the biometric feature, remove the batteries so the fingerprint scanner can't activate, and secure the safe using the physical key only. BBRKIN is offering a free repair kit — contact them at support@bbrkin.com or at securitysafe.store/recallreplacement. No injuries have been reported.

The safes were sold exclusively on Amazon.com and can be identified by the brand name BBRKIN or MouTec on the upper right of the front panel, and serial numbers in the range SQC200034980 through SQC202319171 on the lower right.

Have you checked your gun safe's lock recently? 🔒
facebook.image_caption: CPSC recall: BBRKIN biometric gun safes. Faulty lock, 9,100 units. Stop biometric feature — use key only, free repair kit available. 🔒

article.headline: BBRKIN Biometric Gun Safe Recalled: What Colorado Springs Families Need to Know

stories.title: CPSC Warns Parents to Stop Using Babypark 3-in-1 Carriers Immediately

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
CPSC WARNING: Stop using Babypark 3-in-1 soft carriers (gray, leaf design). Leg openings too large — infants can slip through and fall.

Sold on Amazon through May 2026 (~$27). Company refused to recall. Dispose now. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Babypark's 3-in-1 carriers aren't being recalled — because the manufacturer refused to cooperate.

That makes this worse, not better. A CPSC warning with no remedy means: dispose of it now and don't pass it on. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If you own a Babypark 3-in-1 soft baby carrier — the gray one with the colorful leaf design — stop using it now and dispose of it. Don't give it to another family, don't donate it, don't sell it at your next garage sale.

The CPSC issued a safety warning (not a recall) this summer about these carriers, which were sold on Amazon.com from April 2024 through May 2026 for about $27. The problem: the leg openings on the carrier are large enough that an infant or toddler can slip through them, creating a serious fall hazard. The product violates the mandatory federal safety standard for soft infant and toddler carriers.

The reason this is a warning rather than a recall is that the manufacturer — Beiyou Bless, based in China — refused to cooperate with the CPSC's recall process. When a company refuses, the CPSC can issue a warning but can't force a remedy. That means there's no refund coming, no replacement program, no return process. The CPSC's message is direct: dispose of the product and don't pass it on to anyone else.

If you purchased this carrier or know someone who did — particularly as a gift for a new parent — please share this. With Amazon being the only retailer and the sale window spanning two years, there are families out there who may still be using these carriers without knowing about the hazard.

Do you have a system for checking your baby gear against CPSC warnings? 👶
facebook.image_caption: CPSC warns: stop using Babypark 3-in-1 soft carriers immediately. Leg openings allow infants to slip through. Company refused recall — dispose now. 👶

article.headline: CPSC Warning: Stop Using Babypark 3-in-1 Soft Carriers — Company Refused to Recall

stories.title: Aspen Acres Fire Update: 34% Contained, 780+ Structures Lost — Colorado's Worst Month of Wildfire Damage

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Aspen Acres Fire update — July 11:

• 97,504 acres burned
• 34% contained (up from 15%)
• 780+ structures destroyed
• 7th most destructive in Colorado history

Evacuations remain in Custer, Pueblo, Huerfano. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
If you have family in Custer, Pueblo, or Huerfano County — check in today.

Aspen Acres Fire: 34% contained, 780+ structures destroyed. Mandatory evacuations remain. Hwys 165, 78, 67, and 96 have closures. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
The Aspen Acres Fire continues to burn in southern Colorado, and this week brought both encouraging and sobering news.

The encouraging part: as of July 11, the fire is 34% contained — up significantly from 15% earlier in the week. More than 300 additional firefighters joined suppression efforts, and crews have been working to strengthen containment lines around Colorado City, Rye, and other threatened communities. At 97,504 acres, the fire has been held relatively close to its earlier size, suggesting crews have made real progress.

The sobering part: new aerial analysis from Pueblo and Custer counties shows the fire has damaged or destroyed more than 780 structures — making it the seventh most destructive wildfire in Colorado's recorded history. Three water treatment plants have been affected. Sections of Highways 165, 78, 67, and 96 remain closed.

Mandatory evacuation orders remain in place for portions of Custer, Pueblo, and Huerfano counties. Level 2 warnings are active in parts of Fremont County. If you have family or friends in the area, checking inciweb.nwcg.gov will give you the most current evacuation status and fire perimeter information.

For COS families, the fire is roughly 60 miles to our south. Air quality can be affected depending on wind direction — on smoky days, keeping kids indoors during peak afternoon hours is a good precaution.

Do you know someone in the evacuation zone? 🔥
facebook.image_caption: Aspen Acres Fire: 34% contained, 780+ structures destroyed — 7th most destructive in Colorado history. Evacuations ongoing in Custer, Pueblo, Huerfano. 🔥

article.headline: Aspen Acres Fire: 34% Contained, 780+ Structures Destroyed — Now 7th Most Destructive in Colorado History

stories.title: Summerfest TODAY at Bancroft Park — Free Art, Craft and Music Festival in Old Colorado City

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
TODAY: Summerfest at Bancroft Park, 2408 W. Colorado Ave. 11 AM–6 PM.

Free admission. Free parking. Art, jewelry, pottery, food, live music.

Final day — head to Old Colorado City this afternoon. 🎨 #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
We're calling it: there's still time today to catch Summerfest at Bancroft Park in Old Colorado City.

The festival runs until 6 PM today (July 12) at 2408 W. Colorado Ave — and it's completely free, including parking. It's a genuine arts and makers festival: fine art, handcrafted jewelry, pottery, photography, woodworking, home decor, gourmet treats, and live music across the park. The kind of event where you browse for 20 minutes and end up staying for two hours.

Today is the last day of the two-day event, so if Saturday slipped by you, this afternoon is your chance. It's an easy family outing — no tickets, no fuss, just head down to Old Colorado City and you're in. Bancroft Park is also right near the William Street end of Old Colorado City's shopping and dining district, so you can make a full afternoon out of it.

What's your favorite spot in Old Colorado City? 🎨
facebook.image_caption: Summerfest is TODAY at Bancroft Park — 11 AM to 6 PM, free admission, free parking. Art, craft, food, live music in Old Colorado City. 🎨

article.headline: Summerfest Is Today at Bancroft Park — Free Art Festival in Old Colorado City Through 6 PM

stories.title: Figure Skating Weekend at USOPM — Meet Olympians Jason Brown and Danny O'Shea TODAY

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
TODAY at the US Olympic & Paralympic Museum: Figure Skating Weekend. 10 AM–5 PM.

Jason Brown and Danny O'Shea — noon to 2 PM.

Scavenger hunt, skating artifacts, giveaways. 200 S. Sierra Madre St. ⛸️ #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Did your family make it to the US Olympic & Paralympic Museum today? It was a good day for it.

The museum is hosting Figure Skating Weekend through today (July 12), and if you went this afternoon you might have caught two-time Olympian and bronze medalist Jason Brown or Milano Cortina 2026 gold medalist Danny O'Shea — both available for meet and greets from noon to 2 PM. The day included a figure skating-themed scavenger hunt through the museum, artifacts on loan from the World Figure Skating Museum and Hall of Fame, and information from U.S. Figure Skating representatives about Learn to Skate USA programs in the area.

If you missed today's special programming, the museum itself — at 200 S. Sierra Madre St. — is still a great COS stop any day. The Figure Skating Weekend wraps today, but the museum's permanent collection spans the full history of the US Olympic and Paralympic movements.

The Learn to Skate USA representatives also had information about local rinks and skating programs — a great starting point if your kids came home asking about ice skating lessons.

Did your family visit the USOPM during Figure Skating Weekend? ⛸️
facebook.image_caption: Figure Skating Weekend at the US Olympic & Paralympic Museum wraps today — Olympians, scavenger hunts, and skating history at 200 S. Sierra Madre St. ⛸️

article.headline: Figure Skating Weekend at the USOPM: Olympians, Artifacts, and a Scavenger Hunt — Today Through 5 PM
